name: SWOT + TOWS Analysis
description: Maps internal strengths/weaknesses against external opportunities/threats; then crosses quadrants to generate strategic options.
best_for: Comprehensive situation assessment; strategic direction generation; identifying internal vs external factors

name: PESTLE Analysis
description: Scans the macro-environment across Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Legal, Environmental dimensions.
best_for: Identifying external forces affecting the business; regulatory scanning; macro-environment assessment

name: Porter's Five Forces
description: Assesses structural industry attractiveness by rating five competitive forces.
best_for: Understanding competitive dynamics; industry profitability drivers; supplier and buyer power assessment

name: TAM / SAM / SOM Market Sizing
description: Estimates addressable market at three levels using bottom-up calculation.
best_for: Sizing the market opportunity; setting realistic share targets; investor-ready market estimates

name: Jobs-to-be-Done (JTBD)
description: Frames customer motivation as functional, emotional, and social jobs they hire a product to accomplish.
best_for: Understanding customer motivation; feeding positioning and messaging; customer research

name: Digital Maturity Model (5S)
description: Baseline assessment of digital marketing maturity across Sell, Serve, Speak, Save, Sizzle dimensions.
best_for: Scoring current digital capability; identifying gaps for objectives; benchmarking maturity

name: OKR Framework
description: Objectives and Key Results — qualitative inspiring objective paired with quantitative time-bound key results.
best_for: Setting stretch goals with measurable outcomes; review cadence; cross-team alignment

name: RACE Framework
description: Digital marketing planning model covering Reach, Act, Convert, Engage lifecycle stages.
best_for: Ensuring objectives cover all funnel stages; mapping KPIs to customer lifecycle

name: PR Smith's 5S Objectives
description: Five objective categories — Sell, Serve, Speak, Save, Sizzle — ensuring objectives go beyond revenue.
best_for: Broadening objective scope beyond sales metrics; service and brand objectives

name: Objective Cascade Method
description: Works backwards from business revenue goal through marketing contribution to channel-level sub-objectives.
best_for: Building bottom-up channel targets from a top-line revenue goal; conversion benchmarking

name: STP: Segmentation Targeting Positioning
description: Core strategic sequence — divide market into segments, choose which to pursue, define positioning.
best_for: Defining target segments; competitive positioning; perceptual mapping

name: Moore's Positioning Statement Formula
description: Fill-in-the-blank template for writing a precise, differentiated positioning statement.
best_for: Crafting a defensible positioning statement; differentiation messaging; customer language

name: Ansoff Matrix
description: 2x2 growth strategy matrix across existing/new markets and existing/new products.
best_for: Determining growth direction; assessing risk level; connecting growth strategy to channels

name: Porter's Generic Strategies
description: Three fundamental competitive advantage approaches — Cost Leadership, Differentiation, or Focus.
best_for: Choosing competitive strategy; avoiding stuck-in-the-middle; aligning messaging to strategy

name: Blue Ocean Strategy — 4 Actions Framework
description: Tool for finding uncontested market space by eliminating, reducing, raising, and creating factors.
best_for: Breaking out of commoditised markets; high competitive rivalry situations; strategy canvas

name: Value Proposition Canvas (Osterwalder)
description: Maps business offerings against customer jobs, pains, and gains to ensure product-market fit.
best_for: Ensuring features connect to real customer needs; pain/gain mapping; product-market fit

name: Online Value Proposition (OVP) — PR Smith
description: Articulates why a customer should engage with the brand online versus alternatives.
best_for: Defining unique digital experience advantage; differentiating from offline and competitors

name: Customer Journey Mapping
description: Visual representation of every step from problem recognition to advocacy with actions, thoughts, and emotions.
best_for: Identifying journey pain points and gaps; mapping touchpoints; feeding tactic requirements

name: AARRR Pirate Metrics (Dave McClure)
description: Five-stage funnel model — Acquisition, Activation, Retention, Revenue, Referral — to diagnose growth levers.
best_for: Identifying the biggest funnel leak; prioritising tactical investment; growth diagnostics

name: ICE Scoring (Sean Ellis)
description: Prioritisation framework scoring tactics on Impact, Confidence, and Ease to produce a ranked backlog.
best_for: Ranking candidate tactics into a prioritised execution backlog; sprint planning input

name: PIE Framework (Goward)
description: CRO-specific prioritisation scoring pages/experiments on Potential, Importance, and Ease.
best_for: Prioritising conversion rate optimisation experiments; A/B test planning

name: Hub-Hero-Help Content Model
description: Three-tier content strategy allocating content types by audience scale, frequency, and intent.
best_for: Structuring content production cadence; balancing brand vs search content; editorial calendar

name: Pillar-Cluster SEO Model
description: Site architecture with broad pillar pages and supporting cluster pages, all interlinked.
best_for: Building topical authority; structured content architecture; internal linking strategy

name: TOFU / MOFU / BOFU
description: Content classification system aligned to buyer decision journey — Top, Middle, Bottom of Funnel.
best_for: Auditing content coverage by funnel stage; identifying content gaps; funnel mapping

name: 7P Marketing Mix
description: Expanded marketing mix — Product, Price, Place, Promotion, People, Process, Physical Evidence.
best_for: Ensuring tactics address all 7Ps; service marketing audit; digital translation of each P

name: 70/20/10 Budget Rule
description: Budget allocation — 70% proven core, 20% emerging/scaling, 10% experimental/new bets.
best_for: Allocating channel budget with appropriate risk distribution; balancing proven vs experimental

name: Situational Framework Routing
description: Three-layer router (Brand Maturity + AARRR Gap + Funnel Weight) selecting one of 9 playbooks with channel-framework pairings.
best_for: Determining the right tactical playbook based on brand situation; always read in full during Phase 4

name: RACI Matrix
description: Responsibility assignment — Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed — for campaign tasks.
best_for: Assigning clear ownership; preventing bottlenecks; team role clarity

name: Agile Marketing Sprints
description: Scrum-adapted 2-week marketing sprints with planning, standup, review, and retrospective.
best_for: Structuring execution into prioritised time-boxed work cycles; backlog management

name: Objective-and-Task Budgeting
description: Bottom-up budget built by defining the objective, then costing required tasks.
best_for: Building evidence-based budgets linked directly to outcomes; cost benchmarking

name: Kotter's 8-Step Change Model
description: Organisational change framework for overcoming resistance to marketing plan adoption.
best_for: Addressing leadership misalignment; structural barriers to execution; organisational resistance

name: Attribution Models Comparison
description: Comparison of attribution models — first-touch, last-touch, linear, time-decay, position-based, data-driven.
best_for: Selecting the right attribution model for the brand's data maturity and channel mix

name: North Star Metric
description: Single metric capturing core customer value — if it grows, revenue and retention follow.
best_for: Selecting the one metric that best predicts long-term business health; avoiding vanity metrics

name: Leading vs. Lagging Indicators
description: Pairs leading predictive indicators with lagging outcome indicators per channel.
best_for: Building dashboards that drive decisions; prediction and monitoring; channel-level indicator pairing

name: PDCA Cycle (Plan-Do-Check-Act)
description: Four-stage iterative improvement loop — Plan, Do, Check, Act — at weekly, monthly, quarterly cadences.
best_for: Establishing the operating rhythm for continuous marketing optimisation; review cadences

name: Balanced Scorecard for Marketing
description: Maps marketing KPIs across Financial, Customer, Internal Process, and Learning & Growth perspectives.
best_for: Preventing over-optimisation on financial KPIs; balancing experience and capability tracking

name: OKR Review Cadence
description: Structured rhythm for reviewing OKR progress — weekly, monthly, quarterly, annually.
best_for: Maintaining alignment; enabling timely course-correction; confidence scoring; retrospectives
